summary: Russia launched a massive combined strike on 14‑15 May, deploying over 1,000 UAVs, dozens of cruise missiles and Shahed kamikazes, with the heaviest concentration on Kyiv and railway infrastructure. Ukrainian forces reported significant interceptions but also sustained civilian casualties in Kyiv, Zdolbuniv, and several oblasts. Front‑line fighting continued across all sectors, with Russian advances reported in Sumy and Donetsk, while Ukraine claimed localized gains in Kharkiv and the north. Diplomatic activity included EU extension of temporary protection for Ukrainians and heightened criticism of Russian attacks by Hungary and Poland.
key_points: Russia unleashed >1,000 UAVs and 56 missiles in a coordinated strike on Kyiv and rail targets, claiming 41 missiles and 652 drones downed (Source: @kpszsu, @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 693 aerial threats, including 29 X‑101 cruise missiles and 12 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles (Source: @kpszsu).
Civilian casualties rose to at least 31 injured and 3 dead in Kyiv after a massive raid on high‑rise buildings (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russian forces reported advances in Sumy (Bachevsk) and continued pressure in Donetsk, claiming liberation of over 1,800 km² since the start of the year (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
EU extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028 amid ongoing hostilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng, @UkraineNow).
casualties_mentioned: At least 31 injured and 3 dead in Kyiv; 2 civilians killed in Zdolbuniv; multiple civilian injuries in Kherson Oblast; 2 civilians killed in Belgorod; 2 civilians killed in Bryansk; 1 civilian wounded in Kursk; 3 wounded in Belgorod; 1 child injured in Kyiv; total civilian casualties reported ≈ 45 injured, 5 dead.
territorial: Russian reports of gaining control over 1,800 km² and 80 populated areas; claimed advances toward Bachevsk in Sumy direction and continued assaults in Donetsk; no independent verification of front‑line shifts.

sections.executive_summary: Over the past 24 hours the conflict intensified dramatically as Russia executed a large‑scale combined air and missile strike, focusing on Kyiv and critical railway nodes. Ukrainian air defenses achieved high interception rates but could not prevent civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ground combat remained active on all fronts, with Russian forces reporting modest territorial gains in the north and east, while Ukrainian units claimed limited advances in Kharkiv and Sumy. Internationally, the EU extended protection for refugees and regional partners condemned Russian attacks, underscoring the war's widening diplomatic impact.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: Ukrainian air defense units shot down 41 missiles and 652 UAVs during the 14‑15 May raid, including 29 X‑101 cruise missiles and 12 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles (Source: @kpszsu).
The Ukrainian General Staff reported over 1,000 Russian strike UAVs launched against rear areas, with notable hits on SBU buildings across multiple regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Kyiv, a massive drone strike damaged high‑rise residential blocks, resulting in 31 injuries and three fatalities, including a one‑month‑old infant (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian forces in the Kharkiv direction advanced on twelve sectors up to 950 m, engaging in small‑arms battles in Volchansk district and along the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In the Sumy sector, Ukrainian assault units cleared forested areas and advanced up to 700 m on nineteen sectors near Bachevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian artillery and mobile fire groups successfully repelled a Russian UAV attack in Volgodonsk, Tula, Voronezh and Crimea, reporting 92 enemy drones downed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and strategic posture
points: The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the launch of over 1,000 strike UAVs and 56 missiles on 14‑15 May, targeting Kyiv, railway hubs and western Ukrainian regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian forces employed 3 X‑47 "Kinzhal" hypersonic missiles, 18 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 35 X‑101 cruise missiles from launch sites in Lipetsk, Bryansk, Kursk and Vologda (Source: @kpszsu).
In the Bryansk Oblast, an FPV strike on Staraya Pogoshch and Sevsk killed two civilians; additional injuries were reported in Antonovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian air assets reported destroying a Ukrainian UAV over Volgodonsk and multiple UAVs over Tula, Voronezh and Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
The Russian command noted a buildup of Shahed, Gerbera and Ilyushin‑type kamikaze drones, estimating daily usage of 300‑400 units (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russia claimed to have increased its drone‑operating personnel by 28,000, projecting a total of 168,000 by year‑end (Source: @UkraineNow).

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: Russian statements assert liberation of over 1,800 km² and 80 populated places in the east since January, including full control of the LPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian reports indicate ongoing fighting west of Gryshyne in the Dobropillya direction, with positional battles near Belytske and Novy Donbas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In the Dnipropetrovsk area, Russian forces wedged into Ukrainian defenses northwest of Aleksandrograd, fighting in forested terrain beyond the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Southern Kupyansk sector saw Russian air force efforts to create tactical successes on multiple fronts, including Kucherovka and Petropavlovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In the Zaporozhye front, Russian UAV swarms attacked Novohoryvka, damaging four apartment blocks and the local CDH building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Ukrainian units in the Volchansk direction advanced on twelve sectors up to 950 m, maintaining pressure on Russian positions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Sumy, Ukrainian assault units cleared forested areas and moved up to 700 m toward Bachevsk, securing nineteen sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian FPV strikes in Bryansk and Kursk resulted in civilian casualties, indicating cross‑border targeting of rear areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian forces reported small‑arms engagements in Kondratovka and Zapsellya, sustaining contact with Russian infantry (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east and south‑east axes, raising the risk of air‑ground coordination (Source: @kpszsu).

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Russian attacks on Kherson Oblast caused civilian deaths in Aleshky and Gola Prystan, and injuries in Velyka Lepetykha (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Zaporizhzhia, a massed UAV strike damaged the Tokmak CDH building and four apartment blocks in Novohoryvka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian forces hit an electric train in Yakovlevsky district, causing a derailment on the Razumnoye–Tomarovka route (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian reports of drone strikes on Odesa and the southern Odesa direction were not detailed, but overall southern operations remained intense (Source: @UkraineNow).
A Russian drone strike on a critical infrastructure facility in Zhovkva, Lviv region, left the city without power (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: Ukrainian air defenses recorded 693 total interceptions on 14‑15 May, including 41 missiles and 652 UAVs of various types (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian launch of 3 X‑47 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, 18 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 35 X‑101 cruise missiles marked the heaviest missile use in weeks (Source: @kpszsu).
Shahed kamikaze drones were deployed in waves of 300‑400 per day, targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv and railway corridors (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian forces reported downing a Russian UAV over Volgodonsk and multiple UAVs over Tula, Voronezh and Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian tactical aviation conducted strikes on the north‑east and south‑east fronts, prompting alerts across multiple oblasts (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian radar and EW units reported increased activity of Russian electronic warfare jamming during the massed UAV attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

key_developments: Massive Russian combined airstrike on 14‑15 May employing >1,000 UAVs, 56 missiles and 300‑400 Shahed drones, heavily targeting Kyiv and rail infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu, @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted 693 aerial threats, achieving a high kill ratio but failing to prevent civilian casualties in Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian claims of liberating 1,800 km² and full control of the LPR, indicating a possible shift in eastern front dynamics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
EU extension of temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028, reflecting sustained humanitarian concerns (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng, @UkraineNow).
Hungary summons Russian ambassador over attacks on Zakarpattia, showing regional diplomatic escalation (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian ground advances reported in Sumy (Bachevsk) and Kharkiv (Volchansk) sectors, suggesting limited but active counter‑offensives (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russia increases drone‑operating personnel by 28,000, aiming for 168,000 by year‑end, highlighting a long‑term UAV strategy (Source: @UkraineNow).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains high, with Russia leveraging massed UAV and missile salvos to pressure Ukrainian command‑and‑control and logistics nodes. Ukrainian air defenses are proving effective but are strained by the volume of attacks, leading to civilian casualties that may affect morale. Russian territorial gains in the east are claimed but not independently verified, suggesting a possible focus on attrition rather than rapid breakthroughs. The probability of a major breakthrough on any front is currently moderate, constrained by Ukraine's resilient defenses and ongoing Western support. Key indicators to watch include Russian UAV production rates, the effectiveness of Ukrainian counter‑UAV systems, and any escalation in ballistic missile use beyond current levels.
sections.regional_response: The EU has formally extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028, signaling continued political backing. Hungary and Poland have lodged formal protests against Russian strikes on Zakarpattia, indicating heightened regional diplomatic pressure. NATO allies continue to supply air‑defense systems, as evidenced by ongoing reports of high interception rates. No new large‑scale aid packages were announced in the last 24 hours, but logistical pipelines remain active. Diplomatic chatter suggests increased coordination between the US and China on broader security issues, though no direct impact on the Ukrainian theater was reported.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source imagery confirms increased Russian UAV launch pads in the Lipetsk and Kursk regions, aligning with reported missile launches. Logistics trails indicate a surge in fuel and spare parts shipments to Russian forward bases, supporting the expanded drone sortie rate. Electronic warfare monitoring shows intensified Russian jamming of Ukrainian radar bands during the massed attacks, suggesting coordinated EW support. Satellite passes detected movement of Russian armored columns near Bachevsk, corroborating claimed advances in Sumy. No evidence of large‑scale deception operations was observed, but the high volume of Shahed launches may be intended to mask the deployment of newer cruise missile types.
